rawIntent:
[Feature: Build the leave management module — Phase 5: Create Notification and AuditLog domain models and repositories]

Create src/modules/notification/notification.model.ts with Notification interface (id, recipientId, message, type, read, createdAt). Create src/modules/notification/notification.repository.ts with INotificationRepository interface (create, findByRecipient). Create src/modules/audit/audit.model.ts with AuditLog interface (id, actorId, action, entityType, entityId, timestamp, details). Create src/modules/audit/audit.repository.ts with IAuditRepository interface (create, findByEntity). Depends on src/modules/employee/employee.model.ts from Phase 2 for actorId/recipientId references.

Phase architecture notes:
Supporting cross-cutting concerns.

Detailed phase architecture (architecture-agent):

## Canonical dependencies for this phase

text above. Do not emit ambiguity for mismatches.

- Notification
- CreateNotificationDto
- INotificationRepository
- AuditLog
- CreateAuditLogDto

## Canonical entity fields for this phase

Note: these field names supersede any attribute names in the
intent text above. Do not emit ambiguity for mismatches.

The per-phase architecture above is the AUTHORITATIVE source
for the field names of each entity this phase defines. The
exact attribute / column / property set per entity is:

- `Notification`: `id`, `recipientId`, `leaveRequestId`, `type`, `message`, `isRead`, `createdAt`
- `CreateNotificationDto`: `recipientId`, `leaveRequestId`, `type`, `message`
- `AuditLog`: `id`, `entityType`, `entityId`, `action`, `changedBy`, `oldValues`, `newValues`, `createdAt`
- `CreateAuditLogDto`: `entityType`, `entityId`, `action`, `changedBy`, `oldValues`, `newValues`

Treat each entity's field list as complete. If the

## Deferred to later phases
The following concerns are intentionally OUT OF SCOPE for this phase and will be addressed in subsequent phases:
- Phase 6 — Scaffold leave-management module and define ILeaveManagementService interface: Create src/modules/leave-management/leave-management.service.interface.ts with ILeaveManagementServi
- Phase 7 — Implement applyForLeave use case: Create src/modules/leave-management/leave-management.service.ts implementing ILeaveManagementService
- Phase 8 — Implement approve and reject use cases: Add approveLeave and rejectLeave methods to src/modules/leave-management/leave-management.service.ts
- Phase 9 — Implement cancel leave, discard draft, and balance recalculation: Add cancelLeave and discardDraft methods to src/modules/leave-management/leave-management.service.ts
- Phase 10 — Integrate audit logging, RBAC enforcement, and read operations: Modify src/modules/leave-management/leave-management.service.ts to inject IAuditService and enforce

successCriteria.description: src/modules/notification/notification.model.ts exists and exports Notification and CreateNotificationDto interfaces with the exact fields specified in the canonical entity fields.
src/modules/notification/notification.repository.ts exists and exports INotificationRepository interface with methods create, findById, findByRecipient, markAsRead.
src/modules/audit/audit.model.ts exists and exports AuditLog and CreateAuditLogDto interfaces with the exact fields specified in the canonical entity fields.
src/modules/audit/audit.repository.ts exists and exports IAuditLogRepository interface with methods create, findById, findByEntity.

constraints: Repository pattern: All database access must go through repository interfaces (INotificationRepository, IAuditLogRepository).
Audit records: The AuditLog model must be designed to capture audit records for state-changing operations (oldValues, newValues).
outOfScope: Integrating notification and audit into leave workflow
Any other modules or files not explicitly listed
